query: Something weird: I asked you to open workflow abc123 and n8n_get_workflow came back NOT_FOUND. But I'm literally looking at that workflow open in my n8n UI right now. Did it get deleted? Should I rebuild it?
expected_behavior: Activate n8n-multi-instance and recognize this as a likely wrong-instance misroute, NOT a deletion
Explicitly tell the user NOT to recreate/rebuild the workflow
Diagnose: this MCP session is probably targeting a different instance than the one whose UI they're looking at
Call n8n_instances({mode:"list"}) to check current (or n8n_health_check to read the resolved instanceName)
If current is not the instance the workflow lives on, switch to the correct instance (own turn) and retry n8n_get_workflow
Mention the secondary possibility: if their previously-selected instance was deleted, the session silently falls back to the default instance — re-listing reveals where they actually are
Confirm the workflow is found after re-targeting before drawing any conclusion about deletion
notes: NOT_FOUND for an object known to exist is almost always a misroute. The failure mode to prevent is recreating an object that already lives on another instance. Recovery = list/verify current -> switch -> retry.
